myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, there lived a nymph named Aella. She possessed a voice so pure that even the wild winds would stop to listen. Aella loved to sing to the stars, hoping they would answer, but the vast night sky remained forever silent. 

One evening, a vain and jealous sky god, furious that mortals paid more attention to a nymph's gentle song than to his deafening thunderstorms, descended from the heavens. He demanded that Aella sing only for him in his celestial palace. 

When she refused, declaring her voice belonged to the free Earth, the god cast a cruel curse. He shattered her physical form, scattering her essence into the deep ravines and rocky canyons across the world. "If you will not sing for me," he thundered, "you shall never sing your own song again!"

From that day forward, Aella became the Echo. Bound to the stone, she can only repeat the words spoken by others. Yet, there is profound defiance in her curse. When travelers shout into the abyss, Aella takes their words and hurls them back, a haunting reminder that her voice could never be truly silenced.

Long ago, before the world had filled with the chatter of beasts and men, the great mountain peaks stood in absolute silence. At the heart of the highest summit lived Kaelen, a spirit of stone who possessed a voice of unparalleled beauty. Kaelen sang to the stars, weaving melodies of crushing sorrow and soaring joy, but the vast emptiness swallowed his songs without reply.

One evening, Lyra, a wandering sprite of the western winds, heard his solitary song. Entranced, she wished to carry his beautiful voice across the world, but Kaelen, fearful of losing his only gift, stubbornly refused to share it. Driven by selfish desire, Lyra waited until Kaelen finally slept. She gathered a net woven from moonlight and stole his voice right from his throat, scattering the sounds across the deep valleys and deep canyons as she fled.

When Kaelen awoke, he found himself entirely mute, his ancient heart heavy with an eternal, silent grief. Yet, his stolen voice never truly faded into nothingness. To this day, if you stand at the edge of a mountain canyon and call out into the void, you will hear the shattered remnants of Kaelen’s voice calling back—a lonely phantom forever searching for the throat it once belonged to.
